title: India, China, and the Balance of Power in the Indo-Pacific
description: The hearing examines geopolitical and military issues pertaining to India's relations with China and the United States, explores economic and technology dimensions of India-China relations, and reviews U.S. policy efforts to enhance the strategic partnership with India, and implications of India's relations with China on U.S. interests.
text:
AGENDA
Hearing on "India, China, and the Balance of Power in the Indo-Pacific"
Tuesday, February 17, 2026
Dirksen Senate Office Building, Room 419, and Webex
Unable to see this video? Click here.
Hearing Co-Chairs: Commissioner Hal Brands and Commissioner Jonathan Stivers
9:30 AM – 9:40 AM: Co-Chairs' Opening Remarks
9:40 AM – 11:10 AM: Panel I: Enduring Challenges and Potential Flashpoints in China-India Relations
Tanvi Madan, Senior Fellow, Brookings Institution [Testimony]
Sameer Lalwani, External Senior Advisor, Special Competitive Studies Project; Senior Fellow, German Marshall Fund [Testimony]
Lindsey Ford, Adjunct Senior Fellow, Center for a New American Security [Testimony]
11:10 AM – 11:20 AM: Break
11:20 AM – 12:50 PM: Panel II: Economic and Technology Stakes in China-India Relations
Soumya Bhowmick, Fellow, Observer Research Foundation [Testimony]
Tarun Chhabra, Distinguished Visiting Fellow, Hoover Institution [Testimony]
Chan Harjivan, Visiting Fellow, Duke University’s Margolis Institute for Health Policy [Testimony]
12:50 PM – 12:55 PM: Closing Remarks
